deck.title: Overcrowded Repair Fixture
deck.objective: Exercise the spec-first repair loop with a deliberately overcrowded slide.

slides.purpose: Deliberately exceed the v0 content layout text threshold so Deck Factory must ask OpenClaw to repair the deck spec before rerendering.
slides.title: Overcrowded Slide

items: This deliberately excessive bullet is written as a single overlong thought to trigger Deck Factory's deterministic long-bullet-overflow check, proving that the renderer does not silently squeeze unreadable content into a client-facing slide and that the repair loop must rewrite the spec.
The slide also includes enough total body copy to exceed the v0 content layout threshold, which should force a spec-first repair rather than an operation patch, mock QA pass, or silent fallback.
OpenClaw should shorten this material into a concise, readable set of executive bullets while preserving the fixture's meaning: overcrowded content must be repaired before the deck is approved.
This final sentence pads the body so the deterministic text-length-overflow finding is guaranteed even if line breaks or punctuation are changed by PowerPoint rendering.
